steps.title: Recognize what the x-intercept represents
steps.content: The x-intercept is where the line crosses the x-axis, occurring when $y = 0$. Since $y$ represents the speed in feet per second, $y = 0$ means the speed is 0—when the bicycle comes to a complete stop.

steps.title: Identify what the x-coordinate means
steps.content: The x-coordinate of the x-intercept is the time when $y = 0$. Since $x$ measures time in seconds after braking, this x-coordinate is the number of seconds it took from when Sheila began braking until the bicycle stopped. That matches choice C — C is correct.
